# AX - Agent Experience Design
# How to design websites and web apps for AI agents.
# UX is for humans. AX is for agents.

## Overview
> AX is the practice of designing your web presence so AI agents can
> discover, understand, operate, and recover within it effectively.
> Every website already has an agent experience. The question is whether
> it's good or bad.

## The 12 Principles
- content/principles/01-agents-are-users.md
- content/principles/02-structure-is-the-interface.md
- content/principles/03-context-beats-prompting.md
- content/principles/04-open-ecosystems-win.md
- content/principles/05-every-action-needs-feedback.md
- content/principles/06-recovery-is-mandatory.md
- content/principles/07-discovery-is-part-of-the-product.md
- content/principles/08-auth-is-experience.md
- content/principles/09-memory-and-events.md
- content/principles/10-trust-must-be-computable.md
- content/principles/11-autonomy-must-be-bounded.md
- content/principles/12-accessibility-for-agents.md

## The 15 Primitives
Building blocks of agent experience on the web:
context, access, navigation, discovery, notifications, memory, identity,
feedback, recovery, communication, autonomy, onboarding, social proof,
orchestration, governance

## Other Resources
- content/anti-patterns.md: 25 things that break agent experience
- content/metrics.md: 20 metrics for scoring AX quality (0-40 scale)
- content/stack.md: 10-layer AX architecture
- skill/SKILL.md: Installable agent skill for AX auditing
- research/comprehensive-research.md: 22K word research foundation
- research/agent-perspective.md: 12.5K word agent experience review
